line: GROVE_RESULT: success - implemented resilient saw-core JSONL parser in `crates/saw-core/src/session.rs`

event.summary: success - implemented resilient saw-core JSONL parser in `crates/saw-core/src/session.rs`

line: - Claude assistant records may produce either `ToolCall` or `TokenActivity`; token usage should only be emitted when no `tool_use` item exists in the assistant content array.

items: Claude assistant records may produce either `ToolCall` or `TokenActivity`; token usage should only be emitted when no `tool_use` item exists in the assistant content array.

line: - `<persisted-output>` parsing is safest when scanning past the `Full output saved to:` marker and skipping blank/preview lines.

items: `<persisted-output>` parsing is safest when scanning past the `Full output saved to:` marker and skipping blank/preview lines.

line: - Sidechain awareness needed to be carried into core event structs so later metrics/classifier code can weight subagent activity correctly.

items: Sidechain awareness needed to be carried into core event structs so later metrics/classifier code can weight subagent activity correctly.

line: - Extended `AgentEvent` payloads and `ToolCall` to include `is_sidechain`, and extended `ToolResult` to carry `output`, `stderr`, `interrupted`, and `persisted_output_path`.

items: Extended `AgentEvent` payloads and `ToolCall` to include `is_sidechain`, and extended `ToolResult` to carry `output`, `stderr`, `interrupted`, and `persisted_output_path`.

line: - Kept `SessionRecord::parse(line: &str) -> Option<AgentEvent>` strict and panic-free: malformed or partial JSON returns `None`, while non-signal record types like `progress` and `file-history-snapshot` are ignored.

items: Kept `SessionRecord::parse(line: &str) -> Option<AgentEvent>` strict and panic-free: malformed or partial JSON returns `None`, while non-signal record types like `progress` and `file-history-snapshot` are ignored.

line: - Reused the existing tuple-style `AgentEvent::ToolCall(ToolCall)` model and updated `loop_detector` fixtures to match the richer `ToolCall` schema.

items: Reused the existing tuple-style `AgentEvent::ToolCall(ToolCall)` model and updated `loop_detector` fixtures to match the richer `ToolCall` schema.

line: - “Partial lines are buffered correctly” is satisfied at the parser contract level by returning `None` for incomplete JSON; actual newline buffering still belongs to the future JSONL tailer task, not `session.rs`.

items: “Partial lines are buffered correctly” is satisfied at the parser contract level by returning `None` for incomplete JSON; actual newline buffering still belongs to the future JSONL tailer task, not `session.rs`.

line: - Handled assistant/user/system/progress and other known top-level record types without panicking.
- Extended core event payloads in `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:71` to carry the parsed metadata.
- Updated loop-detector test fixture construction for the new `ToolCall` shape in `crates/saw-core/src/loop_detector.rs:147`.
